✦ Dev Containers for Real Teams Contents Cheatsheet

A BookBank Field Guide · Developer Tooling

Dev Containers
for Real Teams

Not just demos — the practical guide to shipping
reproducible dev environments that your whole team actually uses.

Written in the voice of a patient senior engineer
who has shipped Dev Containers on real teams — not just conference demos.

Prologue

You’ve seen the demo. Now ship it.

Dev Containers promise a world where every developer on your team opens a project and gets exactly the right tools, runtimes, and services — instantly, identically, without a three-page setup wiki that’s already out of date. That promise is real, but the conference demo skips the hard parts: the Dockerfile that takes eight minutes to build, the volume mount that eats your file-watcher events, the Compose stack that works on Linux but not on Mac. This book is the field guide for after the demo. Fourteen chapters, dense and focused, covering everything from your first .devcontainer/ folder to multi-container remote workflows and team-wide adoption patterns. No filler, no fluff — just the things that matter when real code ships from inside a container.

  1. Introduction to Dev ContainersWhat · why · the spec
  2. How VS Code Uses ContainersArchitecture · server · lifecycle
  3. First Dev Container ProjectHands-on · from zero to running
  4. Understanding devcontainer.jsonEvery field · lifecycle hooks
  5. Building Images with DockerfileLayers · caching · production parity
  6. Using Docker Compose for Full StacksMulti-service · databases · queues
  7. Extensions, Settings, and EnvironmentVS Code config · env vars · secrets
  8. Ports, Volumes, and Workspace MountsNetworking · storage · performance
  9. Custom Features and Reusable SetupDev Container Features · sharing
  10. Remote and Multi-Container WorkflowsCodespaces · remote hosts · multi-root
  11. Team Adoption and Repository TemplatesRollout · templates · governance
  12. Debugging, Performance, and Common ProblemsSlow builds · broken mounts · fixes
  13. Patterns for Real ProjectsMonorepos · polyglot · CI parity
  14. Appendix: Reference ConfigurationsCopy-paste starters · full examples
  15. CheatsheetCommands · fields · quick reference
How to use this book Turn pages with the ← → arrows or the buttons below. Tap ↑ to jump back to this Contents page. Every diagram is drawn in CSS and SVG — it renders crisp at any size, even on a phone.
